mnemonics.title: Categories of System Programs
mnemonics.content: FIVE SDPAC: File management, Status information, Disk maintenance, File modification, Programming language support, Application programs, Communication programs

mnemonics.title: Boot Process Order
mnemonics.content: BOOT: Bootstrap program loads Operating system kernel, which loads Device drivers, then Operating system starts, then Tools (system programs) are ready

mnemonics.title: System Programs vs Application Programs
mnemonics.content: SYSTEM = SERVE (System programs SERVE the OS), APPLICATION = ACT (Application programs ACT for user tasks)

mnemonics.title: Programming Language Support Tools
mnemonics.content: CAD: Compiler, Assembler, Debugger - these help CREATE and DEBUG code

acronyms.usage: Firmware that initializes hardware during computer boot process
Visual interface allowing user interaction through icons and windows
Text-based interface where users type commands (like shell)
Set of functions allowing applications to request services from OS

visualTips.visualization: Think of a LAYER CAKE: Bottom layer = Hardware, Layer above = Kernel (chef), Layer above = System Programs (servers), Top layer = Applications (customers)
Think of PULLING A BOOTSTRAP to lift yourself up - the small bootstrap program 'pulls' the entire OS into existence
Drivers are like TRANSLATORS or INTERPRETERS between the OS (speaks general language) and hardware (speaks specific language)
The shell is like a WAITER who takes your order (commands), interprets it, and brings you what you asked for (executes programs)

analogies.analogy: If the OS is a government, the kernel is like the core executive (Prime Minister/President handling essential decisions), while system programs are government departments and agencies providing services to citizens.
Device drivers are like EMBASSY TRANSLATORS - they translate between the host country's language (OS) and the visitor's language (hardware).
The bootstrap program is like the ignition key in a car - it starts the engine (OS), which then allows all other systems (lights, AC, radio) to function.
The shell/command interpreter is like a HOTEL CONCIERGE - you tell them what you need, and they arrange it by coordinating with various hotel services (system programs).
